sections.title: 1. Redundant Computations
2. Caching Opportunities
3. Memory Leak Risk
4. No True N+1 Patterns
sections.content:
The server layer makes one API call per search request (Brave or Nominatim), with caching. The stocks module chains calls (search → quote → candles) but these are sequential within a single request, not looping. No N+1 found.

---

sections.title: Summary Table
sections.content:
| Issue | File | Impact | Fix |
|-------|------|--------|-----|
| `parseUnit` called twice per query | `InstantAnswer.svelte:352,478` | Redundant regex per keystroke | Share result between `detectAnswer` and `unit` derived |
| Set spread in `isBlockedDomain` | `server/search.ts:565` | Array allocation per result per filter | Iterate Set directly |
| `shoppingRank` sort key not memoized | `server/search.ts:715` | Extra `new URL()` calls during sort | Pre-map rank before sort |
| `JSON.stringify` in cache key | `server/search.ts:146` | Unnecessary allocation on hot path | Use template string |
| `hostname()` recomputed per render | `AiAnswer.svelte:12` | `new URL()` per citation per render | `$derived` on sources array |
| No debounce on AI answer fetch | `AiAnswer.svelte:21` | Excess API calls if query updates mid-keystroke | 300ms debounce |
| Unbounded Map growth (lazy prune only) | `security.ts`, `altcha.ts`, `search.ts` | Memory leak on long-running servers | Add `setInterval` sweep |

codeBlocks.code:
<!-- current -->
let kind = $derived(detectAnswer(query));    // calls parseUnit internally
let unit = $derived(parseUnit(query));       // calls it again

<!-- fix: pass the unit result through detectAnswer or memoize -->
let unitResult = $derived(parseUnit(query));
let kind = $derived(detectAnswer(query, unitResult));  // accept pre-computed
export function detectAnswer(query: string): { kind: AnswerKind; unit: UnitResult | null } { ... }

rawOutputPreview:
Now I have a full picture. Here's the analysis — this is SvelteKit (Svelte 5), not React, so I'll address "re-renders" as Svelte reactivity overhead.

---

## Performance Analysis

### 1. Redundant Computations

**`parseUnit` called twice per query change** (`InstantAnswer.svelte:352, 478`)

`detectAnswer()` calls `parseUnit(query)` internally to return `'unit'`, then the component separately calls `let unit = $derived(parseUnit(query))`. Every query change runs the unit regex twice.

```svelte
<!-- current -->
let kind = $derived(detectAnswer(query));    // calls parseUnit internally
let unit = $derived(parseUnit(query));       // calls it again

<!-- fix: pass the unit result through detectAnswer or memoize -->
let unitResult = $derived(parseUnit(query));
let kind = $derived(detectAnswer(query, unitResult));  // accept pre-computed
```

Or restructure `detectAnswer` to return both `kind` and the unit result:

```ts
export function detectAnswer(query: string): { kind: AnswerKind; unit: UnitResult | null } { ... }
```

---

**`isBlockedDomain` converts a `Set` to an array on every call** (`server/search.ts:565`)

```ts
// current — spreads the Set into a temporary array on each call
return [...blocklist].some((d) => hostname === d || hostname.endsWith('.' + d));

// fix — iterate the Set directly (no allocation)
for (const d of blocklist) {
    if (hostname === d || hostname.endsWith('.' + d)) return true;
}
return false;
```

This runs per result, per filter type (ads + trackers = 2 calls), on every search response.

---

**`shoppingRank` sort key not memoized** (`server/search.ts:715`)

The sort comparator calls `shoppingRank(a.r.url)` multiple times per element during sort. `shoppingRank` creates a `new URL()` on every call.

```ts
// current
.map((r, i) => ({ r, i }))
.sort((a, b) => shoppingRank(a.r.url) - shoppingRank(b.r.url) || a.i - b.i)

// fix — compute rank once per element
.map((r, i) => ({ r, i, rank: shoppingRank(r.url) }))
.sort((a, b) => a.rank - b.
